code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    Each triple is rendered as a short clause and the clauses are
    combined with commas. The first clause is capitalised and the
    sentence ends with a period.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a predicate into a readable phrase (without repeating the subject)
    def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        pred = triple.predicate
        # Simple mapping for common predicates to improve readability.
        # For numeric predicates we add the appropriate noun (students, staff, etc.).
        mapping = {
            "city": "is located in the city",
            "country": "is located in the country",
            "state": "is located in the state",
            "location": "is located in",
            "capital": "has capital",
            "largestCity": "has the largest city",
            "river": "is traversed by the river",
            "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast",
            "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest",
            "hasToItsWest": "has to its west",
            "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors",
            "isPartOf": "is part of",
            "nickname": "is nicknamed",
            "rector": "has rector",
            "dean": "has dean",
            "director": "has director",
            "president": "has president",
            "founder": "was founded by",
            "leader": "is led by",
            "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
            "religion": "follows the religion",
            "headquarter": "has headquarters in",
            "anthem": "has anthem",
            "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
            "legislature": "has legislature",
            "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body",
            "patronSaint": "has patron saint",
            "campus": "has campus",
            "staff": "employs",
            "numberOfStudents": "has {obj} students",
            "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has {obj} postgraduate students",
            "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has {obj} undergraduate students",
            "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has {obj} doctoral students",
            "academicStaffSize": "has an academic staff of {obj}",
            "established": "was established in",
            "officialSchoolColour": "has official school colour",
            "outlookRanking": "has outlook ranking",
            "sportsOffered": "offers sport",
            "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given the technical campus status by",
            "motto": "has motto",
            "latinName": "has latin name",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is at an elevation of",
            "postalCode": "has postal code",
            "longName": "has long name",
            "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
            "governmentType": "has government type",
        }
        verb_phrase = mapping.get(pred, pred)

        # Strip surrounding quotes from object if present
        obj = triple.object
        if isinstance(obj, str) and len(obj) >= 2 and ((obj[0] == '"' and obj[-1] == '"') or (obj[0] == "'" and obj[-1] == "'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]

        # Return only the predicate phrase and its object (subject will be added later)
        return f"{verb_phrase} {obj}"

    # Group triples by their subject to build compact clauses
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    clauses = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Build phrase list for this subject
        phrase_list = [phrase(t) for t in group]
        # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last element
        if len(phrase_list) == 1:
            combined = phrase_list[0]
        else:
            combined = ", ".join(phrase_list[:-1]) + f", and {phrase_list[-1]}"
        # Capitalise the subject only once
        clause = f"{subject} {combined}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join different subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0].strip()
    else:
        # Use commas and a final 'and' to combine multiple subject clauses
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Ensure first character is capitalised and sentence ends with a period
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
